svg.title: Band-select fan-out: all 12 ports and the module on each
svg.desc: The PE42512A SP12T band-select switched at exciter level fans out to twelve ports. Each populated port carries a band module: bandpass filter, CGH40010 driver, a power final chosen by frequency (MRF101AN LDMOS for 4 m/2 m/1.25 m, CGH40120F GaN for 70 cm/33 cm/23 cm, a microwave final TBD for 13 cm/9 cm/5 cm), then a low-pass filter to the antenna. Two slices have no amateur allocation (spare) and one port is a low-level dummy/park. Build only the ports your region allocates.
svg.text: Band-select fan-out – all 12 ports and the module on each
PE42512A SP12T, switched at exciter level (cold). Driver CGH40010 (DC–6 GHz) is common; the final is picked by frequency; BPF/LPF passbands per your region (filters.md).
spare – this 1.5:1 slice has no amateur allocation; leave unpopulated
spare – this 1.5:1 slice has no amateur allocation; leave unpopulated
low-level 50 Ω dummy / TX-inhibit park – cold-switch target
microwave final TBD (13 cm / 9 cm / 5 cm)
Band-box colour = IARU region availability (R1 blue / all-region teal / R2 purple / varies grey), matching band-coverage.svg.
Ports are loss-tiered (RF1/RF12 lowest loss = highest bands). Spare slices and the dummy stay unpopulated. Build only your region’s bands. See docs/regions.md.
